messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to be aware of the potential dangers and take proactive steps to protect oneself. Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of sensational or too-good-to-be-true posts, as they may be fake. 2) Verify information by cross-checking multiple sources before sharing it. 3) Use fact-checking websites and tools to debunk false content. 4) Avoid engaging with suspicious accounts or posts that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 5) Report suspicious accounts or posts to the platform's moderators. By following these tips, individuals can significantly reduce their exposure to fake content on social media and protect themselves from potential harm."
,
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to be aware of the potential dangers and take proactive steps to protect oneself. By following these tips, individuals can significantly reduce their exposure to fake content on social media and protect themselves from potential harm."

Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of sensational or too-good-to-be-true posts, as they may be fake. 2) Verify information by cross-checking multiple sources before sharing it. 3) Use fact-checking websites and tools to debunk false content. 4) Avoid engaging with suspicious accounts or posts that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 5) Report suspicious accounts or posts to the platform's moderators. By following these tips, individuals can significantly reduce their exposure to fake content on social media and protect themselves from potential harm.

The following tips can help individuals reduce their exposure to fake content: 1) Be cautious of sensational or too-good-to-be-true posts, as they may be fake. 2) Verify information by cross-checking multiple sources before sharing it. 3) Use fact-checking websites and tools to debunk false content. 4) Avoid engaging with suspicious accounts or posts that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 5) Report suspicious accounts or posts to the platform's moderators.
